content.boundary: Bounded to funding-design mechanisms and their incentive effects; does not address service delivery capacity, gatekeeping procedure, or school-choice/sorting mechanisms directly
content.change_lever: Redesign of funding formula from diagnosis-gated per-pupil weighting toward census-based or needs-based allocation decoupled from individual categorical determination
content.name: Shift SEN finance architecture from diagnosis-gated to needs/census-based allocation
content.system_problem: Hungary's per-capita normative funding historically offered higher multipliers (2x–3x) for SNI-classified pupils, and task-based financing continues to count SNI pupils as multiple pupils for teacher-post subsidy calculation, creating a theoretical incentive structure that comparative evidence (England, Poland, Finland) shows can drive identification independent of true need
